PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-10-2019 Continued To May-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nicholas Jay for Proposed Executor.

The Court questions whether sufficient diligence was done in trying to find any heirs that may be entitled to notice. Mr. Jay stated that Ms. Brown and Mr. McBride submitted declarations, that he does not think his Client would sign a declaration if what was stated isn't true, and that in the will Mr. Tyler states that his parents are deceased and that he has no other heirs. The Court stated that neither of them tell the Court what they have done to find parties related to the either one of the parents or to the Decedent, spoke of the availability of technology to do searches, and stated that the will does not say that his parents didn't have any relatives. Mr. Jay asks what else he can do to support the declarations. The Court informed Mr. Jay that it is not the Court's roll to tell Counsel how to perform due diligence. Mr. Jay stated that he will articulate what steps were taken to support the declarations. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to May 8, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Jay is to make sure that any documents he intends to file are filed by May 2, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
